sections.label: Technique
sections.placeholder: Multiplanar, multisequence MRI of the female pelvis before and after intravenous gadolinium-based contrast. State field strength, sequences, and any diffusion/dynamic series.

sections.label: Comparison
sections.placeholder: Date and type of relevant prior imaging, if available.

sections.label: Findings
sections.placeholder:
Uterus: size, position, zonal anatomy, junctional-zone thickness; myometrium and any fibroids (number, FIGO leiomyoma location, size, degeneration); adenomyosis.
Endometrium: thickness (mm) and signal; for endometrial carcinoma, depth of myometrial invasion (<50% vs >=50%) and cervical-stromal involvement.
Cervix and parametria; vagina.
Right ovary: size/volume, follicles, any lesion. Left ovary: same.
Adnexal lesion characterisation (O-RADS MRI v1): size (mm), unilocular/multilocular, enhancing solid tissue, T1/T2/fat signal, lipid content, diffusion, DCE time-intensity-curve type (low/intermediate/high risk), and assigned O-RADS MRI category 1-5.
Free fluid / cul-de-sac; peritoneal, mesenteric or omental nodularity/implants.
Bladder and bowel; deep infiltrating endometriosis sites if relevant.
Lymph nodes (short-axis, morphology) and osseous lesions.

sections.label: Impression
sections.placeholder: Concise prioritised bullets. For each adnexal lesion give the O-RADS MRI category (1-5), laterality, and maximum diameter (mm). Management implication: O-RADS 2 (almost certainly benign, <0.5%) = benign, follow-up only per indication; O-RADS 3 (low, ~5%) = consider US correlation/gynae input; O-RADS 4 (intermediate, ~50%) and O-RADS 5 (high, ~90%, or peritoneal disease) = refer to gynaecological oncology MDT. When the indication is oncologic, provide FIGO staging (e.g. endometrial myometrial-invasion depth, cervical/parametrial extent, peritoneal/nodal disease). Use only approved follow-up phrasing.

sections.label: Recommendations
sections.placeholder: Evidence-based follow-up or correlation, drawn from approved phrasing, only if indicated.
